title: 10 best herbal teas for jet lag
intro_desc: Herbal teas for jet lag are a type of natural remedy that helps alleviate symptoms associated with jet lag, such as fatigue, insomnia, and disrupted circadian rhythms. These teas are particularly effective due to their adaptogenic properties, which enable the body to adjust to new time zones. The best herbal teas for jet lag include Valeriana officinalis, Melissa officinalis, Passiflora incarnata, Lavandula angustifolia, Camellia sinensis, Zingiber officinale, Ginkgo biloba, Avena sativa, Glycyrrhiza glabra, and Corydalis yanhusuo. If left untreated, jet lag can significantly impact daily life, leading to sleep deprivation, decreased productivity, and mood disturbances, ultimately affecting relationships, work performance, and overall well-being.

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis tea, also known as valerian root tea, is a natural remedy that can help with jet lag. The tea is made from the roots of the valerian plant, which contains a chemical that helps people fall asleep and stay asleep. When you're traveling across time zones, your body's internal clock gets confused, making it hard to sleep. Valerian root tea can calm your body and mind, making it easier to fall asleep at your new time zone. It also helps to reduce stress and anxiety that can come with traveling. This is because the chemical in valerian root, called valerenic acid, acts as a sedative, making it easier to relax and fall asleep. Drinking valerian root tea before bedtime can help your body adjust to the new time zone faster, reducing the symptoms of jet lag.

Lavandula angustifolia tea, also known as English lavender tea, is a great help for people who travel across different time zones. This is because it contains chemicals called flavonoids and terpenes, which can help calm the body and mind. When you travel, your body gets confused because its internal clock is not in sync with the new location's time. This can cause problems like insomnia, fatigue, and stomach upset. Lavandula angustifolia tea can help your body adjust to the new time zone by promoting relaxation and reducing stress levels. It also helps regulate your sleep patterns, which is essential for recovering from jet lag. Drinking this tea in the evening can signal to your body that it's time to sleep, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep. Additionally, the tea's calming effects can also help reduce anxiety and irritability that often come with traveling.

Melissa officinalis tea, also known as lemon balm tea, can help with jet lag. This is because it helps your body adjust to the new time zone. The tea contains a chemical called rosmarinic acid, which makes you feel more relaxed and calm. When you're on a plane, the stress and anxiety from the flight can make it harder to adjust to the new time zone. The rosmarinic acid in lemon balm tea helps reduce this stress and anxiety, making it easier for your body to adjust to the new time zone. Melissa officinalis tea also helps regulate your body's internal clock. The tea contains a chemical called melatonin, which is a natural sleep aid. When you're on a plane, it's easy to get out of your normal sleep schedule. The melatonin in lemon balm tea helps regulate your body's internal clock, making it easier for you to fall asleep and stay asleep when you need to. Additionally, Melissa officinalis tea has been shown to improve the quality of sleep. When you're on a plane, it's easy to get a poor night's sleep due to the noise, discomfort, and stress of the flight. The tea's rosmarinic acid and melatonin help improve the quality of sleep, making it easier for you to get the rest you need when you arrive at your destination. Finally, Melissa officinalis tea can help reduce jet lag symptoms such as headaches and fatigue. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce the inflammation that can cause headaches, and its antioxidant properties can help reduce fatigue. By reducing these symptoms, the tea can make it easier for you to adjust to the new time zone and enjoy your trip.

Passiflora incarnata tea, also known as passionflower tea, is a herbal remedy that can help with jet lag. This is because it contains a chemical called flavonoid, which helps to calm the nervous system and promote relaxation. When you travel across time zones, your body's internal clock gets confused, leading to problems with sleep and fatigue. Passiflora incarnata tea can help to adjust your body's clock by regulating the production of serotonin, a hormone that controls sleep and wakefulness. Drinking this tea before bed can help you fall asleep faster and sleep more soundly, making it easier to adjust to a new time zone. Additionally, passionflower tea has a soothing effect on the body, which can help to reduce stress and anxiety caused by travel, further promoting a good night's sleep.

Zingiber officinale tea, also known as ginger tea, is a good remedy for jet lag. This is because ginger has properties that help to reduce nausea and vomiting, which are common symptoms of jet lag. When you travel across different time zones, your body's internal clock gets confused, leading to these symptoms. Ginger helps to calm your stomach and prevent these feelings of sickness. Additionally, ginger has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce fatigue and tiredness caused by jet lag. It also contains antioxidants that help to protect your body from the negative effects of travel, such as oxidative stress. Drinking ginger tea can help to regulate your body's natural rhythms and reduce the severity of jet lag symptoms. It's especially useful when consumed in the morning, as it can help to boost your energy levels and get you feeling more alert.

Avena sativa tea, also known as oats tea, is a natural remedy for jet lag. This tea is made from the dried leaves of the oat plant and contains a compound called avenacoside. Avenacoside helps to reduce stress and anxiety, which can be caused by traveling across different time zones. When we travel, our body's internal clock gets confused, leading to feelings of fatigue and disorientation. Avena sativa tea helps to regulate our body's internal clock by promoting relaxation and reducing stress hormones like cortisol. Drinking Avena sativa tea before bedtime can help to improve the quality of sleep, making it easier to adjust to the new time zone. Additionally, Avena sativa tea has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce jet lag symptoms like headaches and muscle pain. Drinking Avena sativa tea may help to alleviate some of the discomfort caused by jet lag, making it easier to adapt to new time zones.

Ginkgo biloba tea is a popular remedy for jet lag. The main reason is its ability to improve blood flow and oxygen supply to the brain. When we travel across time zones, our body's internal clock gets confused. This can cause fatigue, headaches, and difficulty sleeping. Ginkgo biloba tea helps to increase alertness and reduce drowsiness by improving the supply of oxygen and nutrients to the brain cells. It also has antioxidant properties that help to reduce inflammation and protect the brain from damage caused by free radicals. Additionally, ginkgo biloba tea has been shown to help regulate sleep patterns and improve the quality of sleep. By taking ginkgo biloba tea, you can help your body adjust to the new time zone more quickly and reduce the symptoms of jet lag.

Glycyrrhiza glabra tea, also known as licorice root tea, is a natural remedy that may help with jet lag. This is because it contains a compound called glycyrrhizin, which can help regulate the body's internal clock. When we travel across time zones, our bodies can get confused, leading to fatigue, headaches, and difficulty sleeping. Glycyrrhizin in licorice root tea may help to adjust our body's internal clock by promoting relaxation and reducing stress. It also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate jet lag symptoms such as swelling and stiffness in the body. Drinking licorice root tea before bedtime may also help to improve sleep quality, which is essential for adjusting to a new time zone. Additionally, licorice root tea has been shown to have antioxidant properties, which can help protect the body from oxidative stress caused by jet lag.

Camellia sinensis tea, also known as green tea, is a good choice to help with jet lag because it contains an amino acid called L-theanine. This helps the body relax and reduces stress. When you travel to a different time zone, your body gets confused and it's hard to fall asleep at night and stay awake during the day. L-theanine in green tea helps you feel more calm and relaxed, making it easier to fall asleep when you need to. It also helps your body adjust to the new time zone by regulating your sleep patterns. Green tea also contains caffeine, which can help you feel more alert and awake during the day, but in a natural way. This can help you feel more energized and focused, making it easier to adjust to your new surroundings. Drinking green tea can also help your body recover from the fatigue and stress of traveling, making it a great choice for people who experience jet lag.

Lavandula latifolia tea, also known as spike lavender tea, is a great help for people who travel across time zones. This herbal tea is rich in a compound called linalool, which has a calming effect on the body. Drinking it before bedtime can help you fall asleep faster, which is especially helpful when you're traveling to a new time zone and your body is confused. The linalool in the tea also helps to regulate your body's internal clock, making it easier to adjust to the new time. Additionally, the tea's calming properties can reduce stress and anxiety caused by jet lag, allowing you to relax and recover more quickly. By promoting a good night's sleep and reducing stress, Lavandula latifolia tea can help your body adapt to the new time zone more easily.
